name: azure-ai-contentsafety-py
description: Azure AI Content Safety SDK for Python. Detect harmful content in text and images with multi-severity classification.
capabilities: Analyze text for hate speech, self-harm, sexual content, and violence categories
Analyze images for harmful visual content with severity classification
Create and manage custom text blocklists for domain-specific terms
Support 4-level and 8-level severity scales for fine-grained control
Authenticate via API key or Azure Entra ID
Support image analysis from file uploads or blob URLs
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation for the Azure AI Content Safety Python SDK. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 security issues detected. The skill provides legitimate instructions for content moderation using Azure services with proper credential handling via environment variables. No executable code or malicious patterns found.
valueStatement: User-generated and AI-generated content can contain harmful material that violates community guidelines. This skill helps you automatically detect hate speech, self-harm content, sexual content, and violence with configurable severity thresholds.
userTitle: Detect harmful content with Azure AI Content Safety
limitations: Requires Azure Content Safety resource and valid credentials
Text analysis limited to supported languages by Azure AI service
Image analysis requires base64 encoding or Azure blob storage URL
Cannot detect context-specific harm that requires human judgment

useCases.title: Social Media Content Moderation
useCases.description: Automatically screen user posts and comments for harmful content before publishing to your platform.

useCases.title: AI Output Safety Filtering
useCases.description: Pre-screen AI-generated responses to ensure they meet safety guidelines before displaying to end users.

useCases.targetUser: Customer support and community platform teams
useCases.title: Chat Application Safety
useCases.description: Real-time monitoring of chat messages to detect and block harmful content in customer support or community chats.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Text Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this text for harmful content: [INSERT TEXT]. Report severity levels for hate, self-harm, sexual, and violence categories.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Blocklist Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a blocklist named [BLOCKLIST_NAME] for my domain. Add these terms: [TERM1, TERM2, TERM3]. Configure text analysis to halt when blocked terms are detected.

promptTemplates.title: Image Content Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this image for harmful visual content: [IMAGE_FILE or URL]. Use 8-severity scale and return results for all harm categories.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Stage Content Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a content moderation pipeline that: 1) Checks text against custom blocklists first, 2) Analyzes remaining content with Azure AI, 3) Applies different actions based on severity thresholds (0-2: allow, 4: flag for review, 6+: block). Log all results for audit.

bestPractices: Set severity thresholds appropriate for your audience and platform guidelines
Use custom blocklists for domain-specific terms that Azure AI may not recognize
Log all analysis results for audit trails and continuous improvement of moderation policies
antiPatterns: Do not rely solely on automated moderation for high-stakes content decisions
Avoid using default severity thresholds without testing against your specific content types
Do not store or log raw harmful content in analysis results for compliance reasons

faq.question: What Azure subscription do I need for Content Safety?
faq.answer: You need an Azure subscription with a Content Safety resource provisioned. The service is billed per 1000 text records or images analyzed.

faq.question: How do I choose between 4-level and 8-level severity?
faq.answer: Use 4-level (0, 2, 4, 6) for simple allow/flag/block workflows. Use 8-level (0-7) when you need finer control over moderation thresholds.

faq.question: Can I analyze content in multiple languages?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure AI Content Safety supports multiple languages. Check Azure documentation for the current list of supported languages.

faq.question: What is the difference between blocklists and AI analysis?
faq.answer: Blocklists check for exact term matches you define. AI analysis detects harmful concepts and context that may not contain specific keywords.

faq.question: How do I handle false positives in content moderation?
faq.answer: Implement a human review workflow for flagged content. Adjust severity thresholds and refine blocklists based on review outcomes.

faq.question: Is API Key or Entra ID authentication more secure?
faq.answer: Entra ID is recommended for production environments as it supports managed identities and avoids storing secrets. API keys are simpler for development.
